observations.summary: Dan lies back down exhausted, his shadow large and hunched, barely recognizable as a boy, indicating his physical and emotional burden.

evidence.quote: He lay back down. His shadow against the wall was enormous and hunched—a shape barely recognizable as a boy.

observations.notes: The description emphasizes Dan's heavy burden and transformation.

observations.summary: Dan has chosen the cold fire of the star over the warm rot of the sea, symbolizing a difficult, ascetic path rather than comfort or decay.

evidence.quote: He had chosen the cold fire of the star over the warm rot of the sea.

observations.notes: This choice reflects Dan's internal conflict and commitment to a harsh truth or power.

observations.summary: The house is divided against itself, mirroring Dan's own divided state, indicating a fractured family or internal struggle.

evidence.quote: The house was divided against itself, and so was he.

observations.notes: The metaphor links Dan's personal conflict to his environment.

observations.summary: Dan did not come to save his father but to test whether the power still held, and the test was costly, evident in his physical exhaustion.

evidence.quote: He had not come here tonight to save his father. He had come here to test whether the power still held, and it had held, and the cost of testing it was written in every tight muscle and leaden limb.

observations.notes: This reveals Dan's purpose and the physical toll of his actions.

observations.summary: Dan's sleep will bring him no more rest than his father's; they are sleeping the same sleep now, implying shared suffering or fate.

evidence.quote: Dan's sleep would bring him no more rest than his father's. They were sleeping the same sleep now.

observations.notes: Indicates a deep connection or parallel between Dan and his father.
